chats.title: Personal Brand & Positioning
chats.next_step: React to one question — does 'post-acquisition stabilizer' feel true as the brand noun, or does 'revenue-systems turnaround specialist' fit better? Pick the anchor.
chats.breadcrumb: Landed two-layer brand insight: WHAT (value creation/turnarounds) + HOW (calm/steady/trusted = the differentiator, backed by 7 yrs of feedback). Pulled 4 proof pillars. Reframed 'too generalist' worry — brand needs a situational NOUN anchor, not more personality. Leading candidate: 'post-acquisition stabilizer' on a revenue/CRM/E&O spike + AI-acceleration layer. M&A team move is a direct tailwind for this anchor.
chats.open_question: Which situational anchor is true — 'post-acquisition stabilizer' vs 'revenue-systems turnaround specialist' — and how does the M&A move reshape it?

chats.title: Dead & Phish Time Machine
chats.last_completed: Verify PINOUT.md GPIO assignments are clear of the HiFiBerry DAC+ Pro's reserved pins before soldering
chats.next_step: Solder the 2x20 stacking/pass-through header onto the HiFiBerry, then wire the 5 buttons and 3 encoders per PINOUT.md
chats.breadcrumb: Pre-solder pin audit complete: PINOUT.md is fully clear of the HiFiBerry's claimed pins (I2C 2/3, I2S 18-21). The 3 stock-deadstream button conflicts were correctly remapped - stop 2->14, rewind 3->15, play/pause 20->9 (9 reuses unused SPI MISO, documented in notes). Encoders + display untouched and clear. Green light to solder the stacking header and wire straight to PINOUT.md.
chats.open_question: Before relying on stop/rewind (GPIO 14/15 = UART), confirm the serial LOGIN console is OFF: raspi-config -> Interface Options -> Serial Port -> login shell over serial = No, hardware enabled = Yes.
chats.agent_can_prep: Generate a component-by-component wiring checklist from PINOUT.md (each button/encoder -> its BCM pin and breakout terminal) plus the raspi-config serial-console disable steps
